objective: Refactor competitor-ads-intel monolith into modular architecture with VendorStrategy interface, preparing for Crawlee integration and vendor discovery.

completed: Monolith (scripts/competitor-ads-intel.ts) reduced from 1145 lines to 285 lines.
New lib/competitor-intel/ module tree created with 1035 lines across 12 files.
VendorStrategy interface defined — enables multi-platform vendor registration.
PpomppuStrategy wraps existing parsePpomppuPost without changing behavior.
Extraction pipeline extracted: image-utils, ocr, vlm, validation, pipeline modules.
Record builder extracted with recordIdFrom, postIdFromUrl, buildRecordBase.
Script CLI interface and output format remain identical (behavioral invariant).
gaps: No unit tests for new modules yet.
VendorStrategy interface is defined but only PpomppuStrategy implemented.
crawler/ and discovery/ directories are empty placeholders (for Sessions 2 and 3).
CSV parsing still bundled inside extraction/pipeline.ts (will move to input source layer later).
No end-to-end verification run performed yet (needs Ollama + Playwright available).

acceptance_criteria_met: lib/competitor-intel/vendor-registry.ts — Registry with URL resolution — YES
lib/competitor-intel/extraction/ — image-utils, ocr, vlm, validation, pipeline — YES
scripts/competitor-ads-intel.ts rewritten to import from new modules — YES
decisions: VendorStrategy interface with 7 methods: contentSelectors(), parse(), isUserContentImage(), classifyAccountType(), pricePattern(), durationPattern(), vendorId.
VendorRegistry is a singleton (export const registry) for convenience.
PpomppuStrategy delegates to existing parsePpomppuPost — parser module unchanged.
CSV parsing stays in pipeline.ts for Session 1; will be abstracted as input source in later sessions.
artifactRoot is passed as parameter to processPostWithRetry/processPostIsolated instead of computed internally.
Existing lib/competitor-ad-parser/ppomppu-parser.ts is NOT modified — wrapped, not changed.
Existing lib/competitor-ad-sqlite.ts is NOT modified — stays alongside new modules.
constraints: Manual execution only; no automated scheduler for competitor intel.
Do not log secrets or credentials.
Publisher/observer systems must remain fail-closed for missing evidence.
JSONL output format must remain backward compatible through all refactoring sessions.
next_actions: Session 2: Install crawlee (npm install crawlee), replace main loop with PlaywrightCrawler.
Create lib/competitor-intel/storage/jsonl-export.ts for backward-compatible JSONL gzip export.
Rewrite main() to use RequestQueue + crawler.run() + Dataset.pushData().
Session 4: Add unit tests for pure functions.
practical_recommendations: Run: npx tsc --noEmit to verify TypeScript compilation.
The VendorStrategy interface is the key abstraction — adding a new vendor means implementing it and registering.
See /parent/marketing-automation/.planning/spec-kit/refs/competitor-intel-roadmap.md for the full multi-session plan.
Cloned repos at /parent/WEB_CRAWLER/crawlee (TypeScript) and /parent/WEB_CRAWLER/crawl4ai (Python) for reference.
Crawlee is the chosen integration target — native TypeScript, same Playwright version.
